1 Is the Government open to considering relevant commercial or
public sector past performance? The government believes public or commercial past performance is acceptable.
2 Can the Government share the current PBES overall system
architecture, including external and internal interfaces, reporting
capabilities, source systems, and data warehouse components or
constructs? Yes, the government can provide OV1 and Network Topology. Currently there are no
interfaces.
3 What data integration technologies or tools are currently
employed, as opposed to manual data integration? Most are manual data integration processes.
4 What are the current challenges associated with system support,
and what are the top functional, technical, and/or PMO
challenges that a future solution should address? Challenges: utilize cloud native technology with PBES. PBES was an on premises system
placed on top of Cloud One and made to work. It did not take advantage of any cloud
native technologies to improve the system. RHEL 8 upgrade. Middleware upgrade.
5 Is there a desire to provide greater flexibility to different user
groups while maintaining a single, customized platform? Yes, its an enterprise system used by planners, programmers and budgeteers.
6 Does the Government have an interest in reducing variability and
enforcing increased standardization across users, processes, or
configurations? Yes
7 How many distinct budgeting methodologies are currently
employed, and which approaches are used (e.g., baseline or
factor
‑
based, zero
‑
based, driver
‑
based)? All the above. Basline budgeting is the standard starting point for most program budget
formulation and also for incremental increases based on basic economic assusmptions
such as inflation. A factor-based approach is also used to determine funding
requirements based on execution (burn) rates for specific programs. Zero-based budget
analysis is usually part of every budget cycle to identify areas to reduce costs and align
future (and current) budgets with strategic goals. Prior year budget proposals and
matched against actual execution of past budgets is also used to support driver-based
budgeting.
8 Are there specific aspects of the financial process—such as
speed, accuracy, standard reporting, or ad hoc reporting—that
the Government would like to see enhanced, and if so, which are
the highest priority? Data integrity is most important. There are minor challenges with data validation that
must be addressed to reduce the manual workload required to maintain accuracy. Recent user feedback support the need for improvements to seamless integration
between database tables, data entry, and reporting capabilities to reduce significant
bottlenecks exists that hinder productivity and efficiency during peak periods of the
budget cycles. Standard reporting exists but there are calls for improvement along with
a more intuitive UI experience.
9 Given that Hyperion Planning is currently projected to remain in
sustaining support through approximately 2034 and is not
expected to receive significant new functional enhancements,
what are the Government’s plans or assumptions regarding the
future evolution of the PPE capability? We'd like to migrate to newer planning products/technology.
10 Has the Government evaluated a transition to a cloud based
environment? If so, what assessments have been performed to
date to determine the feasibility of leveraging Oracle EPM Cloud? Yes, we are assessing the feasibility of leveraging Oracle EPM Cloud. Oracle has done an
initial analysis for the feasiblity of leveraging Oracle EPM Cloud.
11 Does the Government have an interest in utilizing artificial
intelligence to enhance predictive capabilities or to generate
‑
assisted reference outputs? Yes, GenAI
12 Is the current financial process operating in a stable equilibrium,
or is it exhibiting signs of convergence or divergence? The financial processes at the core are mainly solid. Each budget cycle will always
introduce new challenges which are driven by current events and changing political
priorities among other factors requiring constant adaptation. Steady turnover among
analysts adds to the challenges of maintaining continuity of established process
continuity and institutional knowledge. System designs must provide structural
continuity to mitigate process drift (divergence) while retaining the agility required for
mission-critical flexibility.
13 Does the Government have an interest in enhancing reporting
capabilities, such as report books, report bursting, increased
self
‑
service reporting, or greater standardization?
14 Oracle EPM is currently authorized at FedRAMP IL4 (CUI). Since
PBES operates on SIPRNet and requires IL6 or higher
authorization, how does the Government envision meeting these
security requirements, and is AWS Cloud One expected to be the
primary near
‑
term deployment approach? AWS Cloud One is the primary near-term deployment approach; PBES currently operates
in AWS Cloud One IL6.
15 Can the Government provide the current organizational support
model, including internal and external organizations and their
respective roles and responsibilities? PBES SS_RFI 2026
Q&A
16 What documentation is currently in place to support operations
and sustainment (e.g., SOPs, CONOPs), and are these
documents considered current and sufficient to provide adequate
support today? Yes, bidders library.
17 How many support tickets are currently processed per month,
broken down by functional and technical categories (e.g.,
metadata updates, model changes, defects, security
‑
related
issues)? Roughly 185-200 a month
18 On average, how many process or configuration change requests
are submitted per month? 5-10 changes to back-end; Front-end changes range from 185-200 a month
19 What SLAs are currently defined for the system, and what is the
Government’s assessment of performance against those SLA
objectives to date? None
20 Can the Government describe the current help desk ticket
workflow, including average time to resolution for functional, data
integration, and technical ticket types? Tickets are tracked through Jira and have an average resolution time of 10-15 minutes.
21 What percentage or number of help desk tickets are resolved on
the first pass (i.e., first
‑
contact resolution)?
98%
22 Has the government formalized the acquisition approach and
timeline?
23 Has the government completed the migration for CloudOne? Yes, August 2025
24 Will the Government indicate the anticipated inclusive dates of
the Transition In period? How many days are planned for the
Transition In? Not at the moment but plan is 60 days
25 Will the Government consider adding a rating/score above the
current “Meets” (such as Outstanding) to allow Offerors to be
rated/scored for demonstrating strengths such as an innovation,
value add, efficiency, and/or cost savings? Evaluation criteria has not been finalized.
26 Will the Government be providing any labor categories, level of
effort numbers, or historical staffing information, to assist offerors
in ensuring adequate qualified personnel are proposed to support
PBES? We can suggest some labor categories but not all.
27 Can the government provide a breakdown of current LCATs and
quantities by geographic location as well as
government/contractor site? The current LCATs were based on the contractors determination of our needs from
the PWS. SMEs for Planning, Programming and Budget are required. Typically, DRM,
BI and Hyerpion Developers and Engineers are needed. If you would like to submit a
staffing plan, we can take a look and give you feedback. As far as location, Help, SME
and maybe a couple others will be required to work in DC.
28 What Agile framework is currently in use (SAFe, Scrum, hybrid)? Scrumban
29 Are there plans to modernize or replace Oracle components
(DRM, Hyperion, OAS)? Yes, currently in work.
30 What are the current help desk volumes and SLAs? Roughly 185-200 tickets a month
31 What tools/platforms are used for ticketing and knowledge
management? Jira/Confluence/SharePoint
32 What KPIs or performance metrics will define success for this
effort? In-work
33 How does the Government measure user experience and system
effectiveness?
34 The RFI states that the Government is seeking vendor capabilities
for the configuration, operation, maintenance, enhancement, and
cloud migration of PBES. Can the Government clarify the relative
priority among these areas and the expected balance of effort
across them? Currently in the Cloud as of August 2025. Help Desk, Sustainment and Development
would be the priorities.
35 The RFI references Oracle Hyperion, Oracle Data Relationship
Management (DRM), Oracle Analytics Server (OAS), Oracle
Internet Directory (OID), and AWS cloud migration. Are there
additional systems, tools, or external interfaces that vendors
should anticipate supporting? Software that needs to be supported: Oracle EPM 11.2.24, Oracle Analytics Server
5.9, Oracle Data Integrator 12.2.1.4, Oracle Data Relationship Management 11.2.24,
Oracle Database 19c, Oracle HTTP Server 12.2.1.4, Oracle Hyperion Planning
11.2.24, Oracle Internet Directory 12.2.1.4, Oracle WebLogic Server 12.2.1.4, Oracle
Data Visualization, Oracle Essbase 21c, Docker 19.0.3 (updating to Apache/Mellon),
RHEL 7.9 (updating to RHEL 8), and Windows Server 2019. There is no AWS cloud
migration requirement. We fully migrated to Cloud One AWS in August 2025.
36 The RFI indicates PBES operates on the SIPR network. What are
the anticipated security clearance requirements for personnel,
and what proportion of the workforce is expected to require SIPR
access? Secret Clearance (including Help Desk Support) and all required certifications will
be included in official PWS. PBES SS_RFI 2026
Q&A
37 Can the Government provide additional detail regarding the
current PBES technical baseline, including system architecture,
versioning, and the current status of any cloud migration
activities?
38 How does the scope of this effort compare to the current PBES
contract awarded under SBEAS in August 2023? Is this effort
intended as a recompete, follow-on, or new requirement? Yes, this is a recompete.
39 If this effort differs from the current PBES contract, is any portion
of the work currently being performed by an existing contractor? If
so, can the Government provide the applicable contract vehicle
and scope boundaries?
40 If portions of this requirement are new, does this represent net-
new capability or a reorganization of existing PBES support
activities?
41 Given PBES supports Air Force planning, programming, and
budgeting processes, will the Government assess potential
Organizational Conflict of Interest (OCI) considerations for
vendors supporting BES Directorate or related efforts? If so, what
mitigation approaches are anticipated? You will claim any OCI concerns you may and mitigate with a plan. We will also
inform you of any contractor that is involved in our source selection process.
42 The RFI specifies responses should be submitted in MS Word
format. Can the Government clarify whether responses to the
accompanying Excel questionnaire may be submitted in narrative
Word format, or if both formats are required? Yes, will be clarified in the RFP.
43 Are there expected workload fluctuations tied to the Planning,
Programming, and Budgeting cycle that vendors should consider
for staffing? Yes, POM and PB timelines.
44 Can the Government provide insight into the anticipated
acquisition strategy, including potential contract vehicles and
whether a set-aside is being considered? Not at this time.
45 What are the primary challenges or pain points with the current
PBES environment that this effort is intended to address? None at this time.
46 Does the Government anticipate conducting an Industry Day or
releasing a draft RFP or PWS following this RFI? We are in the planning stages but yes, we plan to conduct an industry day or draft
RFP release. PBES SS_RFI 2026
Q&A
